title: Verify the compiler actually vectorized
description: Confirm a loop emitted packed SIMD instructions instead of trusting compiler flags or vectorization remarks alone.
body: Steps: 1. Enable vectorization remarks (-Rpass / -fopt-info-vec) and read why a loop did or did not vectorize. 2. Disassemble the hot function and look for packed ops (vmulps, fmla) vs scalar. 3. If scalar, remove the blocker: aliasing (add restrict), unknown trip count, non-unit stride, or reductions needing -ffast-math. 4. Re-compile and re-check the assembly. 5. Benchmark to confirm the speedup is real. Rollback point: before enabling fast-math, which changes FP results. Validation: the disassembly shows vector instructions and the measured speedup tracks the lane width; a green remark without packed opcodes in the binary is not confirmation.
